entry.title: Can't reschedule Exam
entry.content:
Hello.

2 days ago I scheduled an exam on sapcertification.questionmark.com Due to a technical error I was unable to connect to Questionmark Security - the examiner said that I need to schedule the exam again.
Two days have passed, but I cannot schedule a new attempt, because the site shows the "green clock" sign that the exam is scheduled.
Contacting questionmark technical support (online chat) did not help - they said that I need to contact SAP.
When contacting SAP technical support (sso@sap.com) I received the answer "We are responsible for helping you with SSO related issues."
The site https://learning.sap.com/helpcenter for creating tickets https://lso-ssrv-app.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/ is not working (Internal Server Error)
The online hour is Offline.
The chatbot is not available.
When calling on the phone, there is an answering machine and a polite "call back" after 5 minutes of waiting.

Does anyone know another way to contact learninghub.sap.com technical support?

entry.title: How to increase MRP quantity by including sampling size

We want to include in some cases sampling quantity in MRP output eg:- MRP run give 20 and sampling size is of 1 qty. then purchase requestion should be created for 21 in those cases.

 

BR

Ramesh Kumar

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Conversion – Control Customer and Vendor Legal Entity Merge
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog is about restrict legal entity proposals at transaction CVI_LEDH by implementing a BAdI. CVI_LEDH is used to determine potential customer and vendor to merge them into a single Business Partner. With this transaction you can define, which data  (address, bank data, tax number) should be the leading one for Business Partner creation process in terms of CVI (Customer Vendor Integration).

A detail decription of CVI_LEDH usage you can find at blog post
SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Conversion – Merge Customer and Vendor using Transaction CVI_LEDH (Legal Entity Data Harmonization Report)

Guidance at legal entity merge you will find at blog post
SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Conversion - Merge Customer and Vendor into single Business Partner

Target audience: Functional expert, Development expert
Version: SAP ECC 6.0 or higher

Prerequisites

If you run transaction CVI_LEDH the system determines potential pairs of customers and vendors which are linked via KNA1-LIFNR and LFA1-KUNNR and could be used for legal entity merge scenario.

Very often not all potential pairs shall be really merged into a single Business Partner. In this case it is very difficult to find relevant pairs if the list of potential candidates is very long and and filled with unwanted merge candidates.

Target

This blog describes a way to reduce list of potential candidates. At this example only these pairs should be merged where customer and vendor account group are stored at custom customizing table.
In this example only data with customer account group 0001 and vendor account group 0001 should be merged.

In addition to reduce customer vendor pairs in CVI_LEDH transaction you have to exclude not relevant (not in CVI_LEDH maintained) pairs from legal entity merge scenario in BAdI CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER.

Initial Situation

As you can see at below screenshot there are 4 potential legal entity merge candidates determined by transaction CVI_LEDH.

Following Account Groups are used for example customers and vendors.

Customers 13483 to 13485 are created with Account Group DEBI.
Vendors 100354 to 100356 are created with Account Group KRED.

Customer A0004 is created with Account Group 0001.
Vendor A0001 is created with Account Group 0001.
Remark: it is not mandatory that customer and vendor has the same Account Group key.

At CVI mapping configuration following mapping has been set

For Customer
Cross-Application Components->Master Data Synchronization->Customer/Vendor Integration->Business Partner Settings->Settings for Customer Integration->Field Assignment for Customer Integration->Assign Keys->Define Number Assignment for Direction Customer to BP

For Vendor
Cross-Application Components->Master Data Synchronization->Customer/Vendor Integration->Business Partner Settings->Settings for Vendor Integration->Field Assignment for Vendor Integration->Assign Keys->Define Number Assignment for Direction Vendor to BP

The target is to restrict entries in transaction CVI_LEDH to relevant account groups. Unfortunately this could not done with standard tool like BAdI implementation. If you would do like to do so, you are forced to implement a temporary modification (see bottom of this blog).

Realization

Main part is custom control table and control merge entities by BAdI CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER implementation. Secondary and optional is reducing of entries in transaction CVI_LEDH.

Custom table ZBP_MERGE

I recommend to use a custom table to store relevant customer/vendor account groups. The advantage is, that you can reuse this table to include these relevant account groups for legal entity merge in BAdI CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER.

I just entered a very simple example with only one customer and one vendor account group. At this table you can define your own more complex criteria. In this table customer account group is marked with "C" and vendor account group is marked with "V".

BAdI Implementation

To avoid legal entity merge for mentioned account groups above, you have to skip legal entity merge program logic. Therefore a BAdI implementation for CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER is needed and standard delivery implementation has to deactivated.


Create implementation

Create your own implementation and copy coding from
standard implementation CVI_MAP_LEGAL_ENTITY
method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_CUSTOMER_TO_BP and
method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_VENDOR_TO_BP
into your corresponding methods.

Unfortunately method STORE_MSG is not created automatically. Create this method at your implementating class definition.

Find here example coding for additional lines to standard coding of method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_CUSTOMER_TO_BP
Add lines right after declaration area.

* restrict legal entity merge
data: ls_merge  TYPE zbp_merge.

clear ls_merge.
SELECT SINGLE * from zbp_merge INTO ls_merge
  where accgrp = I_CUSTOMER-CENTRAL_DATA-CENTRAL-DATA-KTOKD
  and   cv = 'C'.

check ls_merge IS NOT INITIAL.
* restrict legal entity merge

Find here example coding for additional lines to standard coding of method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_VENDOR_TO_BP
Add lines right after declaration area.

* restrict legal entity merge
data: ls_merge  TYPE zbp_merge.

clear ls_merge.
SELECT SINGLE * from zbp_merge INTO ls_merge
  where accgrp = I_VENDOR-CENTRAL_DATA-CENTRAL-DATA-KTOKK
  and   cv = 'V'.

check ls_merge IS NOT INITIAL.
* restrict legal entity merge

 

Switch active implementations

Navigate into IMG: Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) -> BAdI: Data Assignment BP <-> Customer/Vendor/Contact Person

Result

Your for exclusion defined account groups will now ignored at legal entity merge scenarion and created as separated Business Partners as you know it.


Optional:
This solution controls only merging based on account groups BUT this will not having any impact on transaction CVI_LEDH which list can be very huge. A solution might be to implement a modification only for CVI conversion purpose. You can modify class CL_CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION method PREPARE_DATA_ALV_OUTPUT. At the end you will find ALV output structure GT_ALV_OUTPUT which you can restrict based on you custom table logic. After conversion you should undo modification.

Unfortunately BAdI CVI_LE_DATA_HARMONIZATION will not help. With this BAdI you have only the option to add customer/vendor pairs based on your custom logic.

entry.title: Re: Not able to preview app in BAS

entry.title: Exciting Opportunity for SAP Partners: Join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative - Cycle 1, 2025
entry.content:
Dear SAP Partners,

We're thrilled to announce that the first cycle of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2025 is still open for your registrations! This is a unique opportunity for you to collaborate with SAP and help shape the future of our products. This is your chance to make a real impact and contribute valuable insights to the development of SAP's upcoming products, features, and applications. Don't miss out – register by March 28, 2025, to join the current projects open to SAP partners. We look forward to hearing your perspectives and ideas on the following initiatives:

ABAP

  • ABAP Cloud Development Assistant
  • OData UI Service from scratch Generator

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Create AI-driven extensions and automations with SAP Build
  • SAP BPM, SAP BRM and SAP Process Orchestration migration to Cloud
  • SAP BTP Cockpit, Cloud Foundry UIs
  • SAP BTP Usability Event - User research project
  • SAP Integration Suite

SAP Customer Experience

  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

Data & Analytics

  • SAP HANA Cloud Elastic Compute Node
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • Fiori First for Sales Order Management
  • Identifying and resolving critical situations in a production planners day in S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • Identity and Access Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Insights into Business processes with Business Event Logging
  • New Financial Reporting for Enterprise Project (Project Booklet) including ETO
  • Planned New Solution for Cost Component Breakdown
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Product Change Management
  • Simplification of transports in SAP S/4HANA® Cloud Public Edition, three-system landscape

SAP Globalization

  • Australia and New Zealand: GST requirement for Down payments
  • Australia: Modified Payment Times Report - AASB 8 and Taxable Payments Annual Report

Lifecycle Management

  • DevOps with ABAP

Others

  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX
  • Shape the future of SAP Cloud ALM Configuration & Security Analysis

We believe collaboration is at the heart of innovation. By working together with our customers and partners, we can reshape the future of SAP products and transform the way businesses operate.

Don't wait – register today on influence.sap.com and join the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2025!

Warm regards,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Re: How can I do a calculation in SAC story to sum values over fiscal periods?
entry.content: Is your model acquired model or live model? If it's acquired model, why you need to have periodic value and YTD value in the two different models? You can put them into same model, or you can just caluclated YTD value on the fly with periodic value.

Just check here:

and check:

 

M.

entry.title: O Captain! My Captain! Agentic AI Leads the Way in the Digital Age
entry.content:
One of the most enriching human experiences takes place in an educational facility. There, students learn soft skills and theoretical background from their teachers, which are the first steps toward becoming a trustworthy professional. Just as students learn and apply knowledge to real-world scenarios, agentic AI systems learn from data inputs to perform tasks autonomously, embodying a modern interpretation of this learning-teaching dynamic. As these systems are designed to achieve complex areas with limited commands, there is a growing demand for tools that learn from input and provide results with thorough reasoning.

Agentic AI Capabilities

Agentic AI combines large language models, machine learning, and natural language processing to carry out autonomous activities on behalf of the user. It is embedded with self-organizing capabilities and self-learning features, which include:

AI-driven support across all case handling stages – It’s intended to be implemented at multiple stages of the case handling process, from information and log collection to triage, troubleshooting, analysis, and action recommendation.

Enhanced efficiency and experience - The goal is to considerably reduce customer effort and engineer workload while improving case resolution times and overall satisfaction.

Automation of repetitive tasks - Repetitive tasks such as writing emails, summarizing cases, and searching for past solutions are automated with AI, allowing agents to focus on problem-solving. Within SAP systems, it can analyze situations, formulate strategies, and execute actions autonomously using SAP Build Process Automation. Just as budding professionals apply tools and methods for learning, agentic AI accelerates task proficiency by providing real-time, context-aware answers to new support engineers. This capability narrows the distance between knowledge acquisition and effective application.

AI-assisted communication - AI-assisted responses help agents craft clearer, more customer-friendly messages, leading to stronger customer satisfaction. AI-generated responses also allow agents to maintain the right tone and empathy in emotionally charged cases and helps non-native speakers communicate more clearly.

Proactive and preventative support - AI is used to detect and resolve system issues proactively. Preventative Customer Care is expanded across all products and integrated with PARIM to bring a true end-to-end Preventative Risk Identification, Risk Mitigation, and Risk Tracking solution.

Flexible architecture - A flexible architecture is in place for consumption of the upcoming SAP Agentic Framework. Based on perception, decision-making, learning, and action components, it enables adaptation over time through implementation of safety measures through monitored performance.

Continuous improvement - There is an evaluation of current existing data, data pipelines, and AI Services to identify areas of growth and continuous improvement.

Best Next Action recommendation - Agentic support offers engineers the ability to get Best Next Action recommendations from a chain of agents and trigger agents at will using prompts to solve cases. The overarching aim is to introduce AI and automation to offer intelligent insights and reduce engineer effort on case resolution. It doesn’t replace human support but rather augments their capabilities, making the learning-teaching dynamic more effective and efficient in the digital age.

Smart response capability - The implementation of smart response capability offers AI-generated responses to selective customer cases, to reduce the volume of cases processed by engineers and enable deflection of cases through Smart Respond. The aim is to auto-respond to selective cases, and constantly increase the proportion of cases that we can Smart Respond to by deriving the confidence from responses offered by engineers to trivial cases, where they are using the Best Next Action recommendations, as-is.

While generative AI primarily creates content, agentic AI focuses on decision-making and actions, making it action-oriented. Furthermore, this customer-centric approach provides nuanced, conversational support beyond traditional chatbots, and offers personalized recommendations and experiences.

The Path Ahead for Agentic AI Integration

The foresight looks promising, with developments for agentic AI in SAP products that encompass:

  1. Connection of support agents with self-healing and reasoning agents to fix issues automatically.
  2. Improvement of natural language interfaces with SAP products, allowing real-time insights about business processes.
  3. Integration with emerging technologies like Internet of Things and blockchain.

As in an educational system, agentic AI is set to learn from data to make informed decisions. At SAP Sapphire 2025, to take place in Orlando and Madrid, we’ll showcase how our agents identify and mitigate risks to prevent business impact to customers, as we point towards a future of seamless integration between learning and problem-solving skills.

A special thanks to Bharath Ramamurthy, Vice President of Strategic Initiatives, Customer Support, and Tarun Luthra, Global Head of Technical Support, Industries & Customer Experience, SAP experts who have contributed to this article.

entry.title: Migration of On-Going Project
entry.content:
Hi All, 

I need to migrate aroud 500 on going project & their associated WBS elements ( total WBS are 30000 ) together with budget to a new system . Can any one please suggest whether we can develop a program to do this activity ? Is there any BAPI to address this requirement as doing this activity is not feasible ?

Thanking you .

Best Regards 

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: How to schedule SAP certification exam without Learni...
entry.content: You can still do certification without subscribing for SAP Learning hub. SAP still allows one attempt and 6 attempt options.

entry.title: Re: Unable extend standard cds view I_WBSElementData_2
entry.content: what are you talking?

entry.title: Introducing Calendar View in SAP Revenue Growth Management: A Visual Leap for Strategic Planning
entry.content:
1. An Introduction to Calendar View 

In the fast-paced world of the consumer products industry, Key Account Managers (KAMs) juggle planning, executing trade promotions, tracking budgets and financial liabilities, and negotiating with their customers to align sales strategies and promotional tactics — often while battling time constraints and fragmented tools. To help address these challenges, SAP Revenue Growth Management (RGM) has developed an easy-to-use calendar view. This feature is designed to simplify trade promotion planning by replacing manual spreadsheet workflows with a visual, dynamic timeline – showing a promotion plan for short-term promotions and long-term pricing agreements over time. Built with input from multiple co-innovation partners, this feature aims to empower KAMs and Sales VPs alike. Let’s explore what makes this feature a game-changer. 

 

2. An Overview of SAP Revenue Growth Management 

SAP Revenue Growth Management is a cloud-native consumer products industry solution for trade promotion planning, management, and execution, serving as the strategic replacement for SAP Trade Management for trade promotion in consumer products. 

The current capabilities of SAP Revenue Growth Management include:  

  • Customer account plan creation 
  • Short- and long-term promotion creation and copying with configurable tactics and spend types 
  • Multi-level promotion planning 
  • Financial liability calculation from manual volume uplift forecasting 
  • KPI Tracking of volume, revenue and margin KPIs 
  • Embedded account profit and loss analytics for volume, revenue and margin 
  • Fund management for fixed funds 
  • Customer assortments 
  • Baseline volume planning with imports from spreadsheets or files 
  • Baseline building blocks for baseline adjustments 
  • Integration with SAP S/4HANA for product, customer and pricing master data 
  • Pricing condition generation in SAP S/4HANA condition contract management 
  • Claims and settlement with SAP S/4HANA Settlement Management 

Financial execution is managed through SAP S/4HANA Condition Contracts and Settlement Management. SAP Revenue Growth Management has been developed from the ground-up to use the native capabilities that are available within SAP S/4HANA for integration with the Order to Cash process.  

SAP Revenue Growth Management has an active roadmap and delivers enhancements and new features every month. One of the recent feature additions is the inclusion of a calendar view.  

 

3. Calendar View in Action: Three Pillars of Smarter Trade Planning

3.1 From Chaos to Clarity: How Calendar View Empowers Planning  

For time-strapped KAMs, Calendar View is more than a visualization—it’s a productivity engine that replaces fragmented systems with a unified, timeline-driven workspace. By complementing traditional list formats, SAP Revenue Growth Management’s Calendar View offers a highly customizable and engaging representation of sales strategies, designed to streamline trade planning. 

  • Tailored Views for Precision Planning 

KAMs can toggle effortlessly between list and calendar views, adjusting time periods (monthly, quarterly, yearly) to match business cycles. Filters for products, trade-spend type, status, and promotion type allow users to hide clutter (e.g., “today” lines and sell-in periods) and be laser-focused on their goals. Example: A beverage KAM can isolate Q1 promotions for “Energy Drinks” to track promotion activities for a specific retailer. 

  • Quick-Edits for Faster Execution 

Hover over any promotion bar to see key details or click “View” to dive deeper into the promotion details and edit or amend the promotion. While direct drag-and-drop edits are planned for future releases, the current design prioritizes speed and simplicity to reduce time spent switching between tools. 

   

3.2 Modernizing Spreadsheet Workflows: A Visual Tool for KAMs 

SAP’s user research has indicated that a lot of KAMs still rely on self-built spreadsheet calendars due to rigid or manual-first systems—even when trade management tools offer calendars. SAP Revenue Growth Management’s Calendar View bridges this gap by replicating spreadsheet-like flexibility while integrating enterprise-grade accuracy and connection to definitive sources of master-data. 

  • Configurable Visualizations 

Promotions are displayed as horizontal, color-coded bars, with differentiation based on type or status. This mirrors the adaptability of spreadsheets but empowers KAMs to display information relevant to their specific promotion and unique business.  

  • Streamlined Navigation 

Built for user experience first, the calendar clarifies key promotional concepts (e.g., sell-in time window and in-store execution periods) through intuitive design. KAMs can group events by product hierarchy to view and manage promotions with real-time ERP data, eliminating manual updates.  

 

3.3 From Planning to Persuasion: A Strategic Tool for Stakeholder Alignment 

The calendar isn’t just for planning—it’s a strategic asset for aligning teams and retailers. By transforming data into visual narratives, KAMs can drive consensus and track progress without system-hopping. 

  • Export for Impact 

KAMs can export filtered calendar views (e.g., “Q3 Frozen Foods Promotions”) to print-ready PDFs, turning complex data into stakeholder-friendly visuals. This facilitates effective communication and targeted negotiations with retailers. Future updates may expand export formats, such as spreadsheets for stakeholder alignment.  

  • Effective Performance Tracking 

Exported PDFs serve as offline progress snapshots for KAMs to track and review promotion effectiveness. 

  

4. Call to Action: Co-Innovate with Us to Shape the Future of Planning 

The Calendar View isn’t just a feature—it’s how we empower KAMs to turn complexity into clarity. While the March 2025 release of SAP Revenue Growth Management focuses on core functionalities, your co-innovation feedback will prioritize future enhancements.  

We want to continue to enhance the calendar view of SAP Revenue Growth Management to be the best possible tool for promotion plan visualization and to actively aid KAMs to make better promotional decisions and find opportunities to grow revenue with their customers. To do this – we want your feedback, suggestions and ideas! 

If your team struggles with: 

  • Disconnected tools fragmenting plans and delaying decisions   
  • Static, non-visual workflows hindering promotion timelines  
  • Days wasted aligning stakeholders via spreadsheets 
  • Financial inaccuracies in forecasting or discrepancies in the application of promotional pricing 

Let’s collaborate. Our Calendar View drives results by: 

  • Unifying plans in real time to close insight gaps and accelerate decisions, 
  • Replacing static lists with intuitive timelines to clarify promotions, 
  • Transforming spreadsheets into visual stories to align stakeholders faster. 

Be among the first to co-innovate with SAP Revenue Growth Management. We're actively partnering with early adopters like you to enhance it. Email RGM_Engage@sap.com to share your feedback, and together, we can shape the future of trade promotion planning.  

 

Final Note: SAP Revenue Growth Management’s calendar view launches in March 2025. Secure your spot as a co-innovation partner today and let’s turn planning headaches into strategic wins.

entry.title: Introduction to Group Management in SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
With the latest enhancement of SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, the Group Management feature is now available. This feature simplifies the user access management to AEM by providing a access grouping mechanism for external identify services (e.g. SAP IAS) . In this blog, I will explain step by step how to set up and use Cloud Group Management.

Why Group Management?

Previously, administrators had to manually create user & user roles within SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, which quickly became consuming and not always aligned with customer user access guidelines . With Cloud Group Management, you can logically group access permissions, optimizing management and security settings.

 

Step-by-Step Guide to Setup

1. Create a Cloud Group

  1. Log in to the to your Advanced Event Mesh service.

  2. Navigate to Account Details, User Management to User Groups section.

  3. Click on Create New Cloud Group and define the groups you would like to use

In my case I have created a "Viewer" role only, which will allow users to access the Event Portal, the Broker settings and the Datadog, but not to perform any changes.

2. Manage Claim Mapping

After you create user groups, you can map claims from your IdP to the groups. Align claims from your identity provider to your available user groups to automatically assign roles to group members. 

  1. Navigate to Group Management 

  2. Add Claim_Value and the defined role from section 1

(Optional) Select Enable Just-In-Time provisioning to allow users who are able to authenticate with the identity provider to log in to Advanced Event Mesh and be added to a group without being manually invited first.(Optional) Select Customize Default User Group and select a user group from the Default User Group drop-down list. When selected, if no defined claim mappings match the user claim value, the user is added to the specified group. When not selected, if no claim value for the user is mapped to a user group, the user is denied access to advanced event mesh.

3. Manage User & Permissions in IAS

  1. In SAP IAS, ensure that you create a group with exactly the same group name defined in AEM.

  2. Assign group to users, which should get "Viewer" access. In my case I take a personal mail address.

4. Access and Check the role

Time to test, access your AEM application. 

And double check your account and the assigned roles

 

With Cloud Group Management in SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, managing user access becomes significantly more efficient. Groups simplify organization, enhance security, and reduce administrative effort. Try it out and integrate it into your event-driven architecture!

Do you have any questions or comments? Feel free to share them in the comments!

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to create a dropdown in Fiori Elements...

entry.title: Leading & Trailing Spaces - Custom Fields - Advanced Workflow
entry.content: To ensure that no leading or trailing spaces are entered by a user in custom fields, what is the best approach? A regular expression prevented spaces between numbers but did not prohibit leading or trailing spaces.

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance (Supplier or Customer) - Creation of Contact Persons
entry.content:
Good Day, valued SAP Experts,

we have a requirement to create contact persons in a dedicated Business Partner Gruping (Z****). We are Using MDG on SAP S/4 HANA. Creation of Contact Persons together with the Main Business Partner (Customer or Supplier) works fine, however Contact Persons are always created in the same BP-Grouping as the main Business Partner.

 

Scenario Despription

While creating our Main Business Partner, MDG allows to simultaneously create a contact person (similar to the Fiori App Manage BP). This works fine. If we are now creating a new Customer or Cuspplier (or both) in the Business Partner Grouping (Z1**), we can simultaneounsly a contact person. That works fine. But the Contact person is also created with the same Busness Partner Grouping (Z1**).

The requirement, however, is that all contact persons are created in one single Business Partner Grouping (ZX**), so independant of the Grouping of the Main Business Partner.

We can not find any customizing to support this requirement. Did anyone have the same requirement in the past and how did you solve it (ideally of course in or close to SAP standard).

Any Feedback is well appriceiated.

Best Regards

 


Acram

entry.title: Redefine S/4 Purchase Order OData Service with Custom Purchase Order Form
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to create a custom Purchase Order bases on the SAP Standard. The custom purchase order needs to get data from a Z-table and therefore SAP Standard OData(FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV) service would need to be redefined as well. I have done the following:

1) Created a new OData Service(ZFDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV) and Redefined the SAP Standard OData Service for Purchase order into it (FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV).

2) I have created an defined and activated a service in the gateway for the new service.

3) I have created my custom form template and uploaded it to Output Management Form Template. However at this step, my custom OData service is not available to select in the drop down?

4) As per note https://me.sap.com/notes/0003009083  I have created a copy of the call back class ZCL_MM_PUR_PO_OUTPUT_CALLBACK and replaced the hardcoded FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV with ZFDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV.

5) I have configured all the OPD steps. 

So my new form is being called correctly and I am happy about that. Hoever.. my new Z Odata service with its DPC classes is not being called. It is still calling the FDP_EF_PURCHASE_ORDER_SRV service with its classes to populate the form. What am I missing? How do I link the redefined OData Service to my custom template?

Kind Regards

Deon

entry.title: Re: SAP Enterprise Integration Strategy - Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M)

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Creating a custom AI agent on the BTP

But if Node.js provides a REST-API then this could be made available here by wrapping it in a Python function. Maybe the whole AI agent could also be created in Node.js actually. In addition to the LangGraph.js you posted, we also have SAP Cloud SDK for AI on pnpm, which can use LLMs on the Gen AI Hub.

entry.title: invoking Unbound action without param dialog in v4
entry.content:
I would like to call the unbound action during the load of object page. I tried below:

oController.editFlow

.invokeAction("CouponManagementUIService.EntityContainer/isCouponCodeExistsUnbound(...)", {

model: oController.editFlow.getView().getModel(),

skipParameterDialog: true,

parameterValues: [

{ couponId: "9b881d7e-f1ec-4b11-8f00-1baece8343ca"}

]

})

.then(function (response) {

if(response.value===false){

console.log(response.value);

}

else

// enableDisableCouponTypeField();

 

}.bind(oThis));

 

The coupon Id is already known on the page and will pass it already. So we do not wanna show the popup dialog to enter the param value. I used the skipParameterDialog but it still shows the dialog.

How can i avoid that ?

entry.title: how to create cube?
entry.content: hello We have been helping clients to enable WFA recently, how to create cube?

entry.title: Re: how to create in SAP C4C a report that measures the d...
entry.content:
I recommend using the data source "Opportunity History" and calculating the time difference in all phases by using the Validity From Date and Validity To Date fields. Please note that fields with the same name are available both in the Key Figures list and in the Characteristics list, so make sure you are selecting the correct ones when setting up your calculations.

Keep in mind that the end date for the last phase is calculated from the date of its assignment until 30.12.9999 (#). Therefore, it is better to calculate the difference up to the current date, and if it is the last phase, use this difference in days instead of Validity To Date - Validity From Date.

Hence, the calculation process can be as follows:

  1. Create a calculated key figure "Period days" as Validity To Date - Validity From Date.
  2. Create a calculated key figure "Period to current" as Current Date - Validity From Date.
  3. Create a calculated key figure "Period If Last Status" based on Period to current, if Validity To Date is greater than tomorrow.
  4. Create a calculated key figure "Period If Not Last Status" based on Period days, if Validity To Date is less than tomorrow.
  5. Create a calculated key figure "Total Days in Phase" as Period If Last Status + Period If Not Last Status.

entry.title: Re: S4 Hana cloud - Retrieve storage location address in an API
entry.content:
Hi Tom,

I am not finding the API to retrieve Plant, storage location ID and Name from S4 HANA Cloud.
Can you please share the details on how you retrieved this data. Which API have you used?

entry.title: Release of SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2201
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the release of the latest Stack 2201 update, featuring new templates for Taxonomy version 6.8! 

Key Changes and Enhancements

Mandatory "Business Tax ID Number" for Shareholders

Starting with Taxonomy version 6.8, the position "Business Tax ID Number" for shareholders, identified as [genInfo.company.id.shareholder.WID], is now mandatory. This change ensures that all necessary tax information is accurately reported, enhancing the integrity of your tax filings.

Updated ERiC Software Component

We have also updated the ERiC software component to version 41.2.6.0. This new version fully supports test filings with the Taxonomy version 6.8, ensuring that you can prepare and submit your tax filings accurately and efficiently. 

New Templates for Taxonomy Version 6.8

Along with these updates, we have created new templates specifically for taxonomy version 6.8.

entry.title: Re: 🚀 SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro – SAP Analytics Cloud! 🌍
entry.content: A palestra foi ótima e com muita intereção de todos! Parabens!

entry.title: How To Show Default Measures on Checkbox as Selected
entry.content:
Hi Everyone, 

After the Story 2.0 came into our life, it became much more easier to do great flexible reports as a SAC developer. However when the SAC technology get developed, also customer demands get developed too. For the old times measure input control and dimension input control was enough for the customer to change one measure or dimension on a widget for furthermore they could have use data analysis but after scripting involved on the reports, they can add or remove many dimension or measures from the widgets with checkbox groups. I use this on some detail tables then customer can change the table dimension and measures but there was a problem. For the initialization part I could select default dimension on rows or column with the function of getDimensionsOnRows() or getDimensionsOnColumns() but there is not a function for measures like this. In this blog we will see how to define default measures on table. 

 

for example, below script that we need to write on page's initialization. I want to add measures of Table_1 into the checkboxgroup_2 and select default measures as selected keys. 

//Measures
var measures = Table_1.getDataSource().getMeasures();


if (measures.length > 0) {
 	for (var a=0;a<measures.length; a++){	
		
				CheckboxGroup_2.addItem(measures[a].id,measures[a].description);		
	}
}

filtervariable = Table_1.getDataSource().getDimensionFilters(Alias.MeasureDimension)[0];
// here is the key part that we get first filter on measure with this script.

var memberid = cast(Type.MultipleFilterValue, filtervariable);
// here is the second key part that we can change type of this measure_table varible to MultipleFilterValue and that way we can choose values from it.

CheckboxGroup_2.setSelectedKeys(memberid.values);

And the result is as in the screenshot. We can show as ticked the default measures in the checkbox group as selected. 

Thanks for reading. If this post helped you or you just liked it, you can give a kudo that will give make me feel great 🙂

entry.title: Expiry Date Adjustment Upon HU Unpacking in EWM
entry.content:
We have a business requirement regarding the handling of HU-managed and batch-managed articles in SAP EWM.

Business Scenario:

  • We manage an article in SAP EWM with HU and batch management.
  • The HU is stored in unrestricted stock with an expiry date of one year.
  • During our warehouse operations, we perform picking or repacking by splitting the HU into one or multiple new HUs.
  • When we execute the first picking operation, we must unpack the HU to take a partial quantity.
  • Once the HU is unpacked, its expiry date must be automatically reduced to one week (as an example).

Example Use Case:

  • Consider a milk bottle:
    • Before opening, its expiry date is 6 months.
    • Once opened, its remaining shelf life is only 3 days.
  • We need SAP EWM to automatically adjust the expiry date of the unpacked HU according to predefined rules.

Question:

Is there any SAP standard functionality that allows the system to automatically adjust the expiry date of an HU once it is unpacked?
If not, do you recommend any enhancements or workarounds to handle this requirement?

entry.title: Re: invoking Unbound action without param dialog in v4
entry.content: tried but still same behaviour. Any other idea ? Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to run BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_SPLIT_DEC without commit?
entry.content:
The warehouse have a delivery, they need to post.

They send me a odata with list of documents ( materials, batches, quantity ). I convert it to apab data.

But if they dont have a material, quantity is not enough - they split the delivery.

So i need to

1)split a delivery, based on data i recieved from app(odata).

2)delete nullabale items in both deliveries, cause bapi_outbound_delivery_split_dec doesnt do it

3)change batches if it's not the same as in main delivery.

4) pack the main delivery and send a message to sapmail that delivery was splitted.

And if there is an error in one of these steps i dont want  to do any changes into db

entry.title: 401 Unauthorized error in HTTPS sender adapter in sap cpi
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In Integration flow , i configured the HTTPS sender adapter. I deployed the iflow and got endpoint in Monitoring tile. I used postman to push the endpoint . Its shows " 401 unauthorized". The credential is my S-User ID which i login the cockpit account.

How i design my Integration Flow?

Sender -HTTPS(Send PO ID in Body tab via Postman)
Receiver - IDOC
Integration process - Add Content Modifier (Get PO ID in Property). Retrieve details in HTTPS in External Call .Using Message Mapping to create IDoc format (Target- IDoc).

 

Please help to solve this issue .

entry.title: Re: What's New in SAP Analytics Cloud Modeling Extensions & Integration QRC2 2025 Release
entry.content:
Hi @mdelibasic ,

Thanks for your question! The Data Import API currently supports the import of Master (including local dimensions) and Fact Data on models stored in a Datasphere workspace.  It also supports the import of currency to currency rates tables stored in Datasphere and with the QRC2 release, it will support the import of Units to the Standard Unit and Unit Description tables.  More features will come with the APIs and Datasphere in the future.

Best regards,

Amanda

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Technology Behind SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) - Out of the box 'Insight App&&

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG Validation Rules: New BRF+ expression types supported for Export/Import
entry.content:
Thanks for sharing this information, Steffen!

Is there a way to export rules, where we have Table Operations? I am still facing issues when I attempt to export:

Rule including BRFplus object RLTYP of type Data Object (Element) is not supported.

Rule including BRFplus object BUKRS of type Data Object (Element) is not supported.

 

And the one that causes the most issues now:

Rule including BRFplus object BP_ROLE_VENDOR of type Expression (Expression Type "Table Operation) is not supported.

If there is a table operation inside a rule, this always returns an error. Is there a fix or a way to export such rules?

 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to schedule SAP certification exam without Learni...
entry.content: Maybe I am misunderstanding the comment above, but this seems not completely right... you can still buy certification voucher packages in the training shop. There is a single attempt package (CER001) and a 6 attempt package (CER006) available there. You can only use three attempts per certification code. Trust this helps.

entry.title: Custom Code Migration (BTP): Object Navigation missing
entry.content:
Hello,

We are using the Custom Code Migration App in the BTP. In the list of findings the objects are named but they are all disabled for navigation (greyed out) and therefore it's not possible to find the specific code segment in these objects that need to be adjusted. This is case for every finding.

 

Any information on why the navigation is disabled or how to alternatively find the affected code segments would be greatly appreciated.

Best Regards

entry.title: Intermittent Login Issues with Trainer
entry.content:
Dear Enable Now Community,

We’ve been experiencing intermittent login issues with the trainer over the past few days. Sometimes the login works fine, but other times, after entering the credentials, the system prompts for them again without successfully logging in.

I’ve verified that the latest version of the trainer is activated, and the link is correct, as we have been using it without issues for several months. Additionally, we are not using SSO for authentication. Right now, the login is working, but I’m unable to ensure it will continue to function in the coming hours or minutes, which is quite concerning.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or can provide insights into what might be causing this behavior?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards

Funda

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized error in HTTPS sender adapter in sap...
entry.content:
Hello,

Do you assign already the role "MessagingSend" to your user?

If you are using SAP Cloud Integration on Cloud Foundry then you probably have noticed you cannot add the default ESBMessage Send (MessagingSend) role to your user.

You will need to create a role collection to support this. 

Add this new role collection the role “MessagingSend” and assign this role collection to your user..

Also you need a instance to Service "SAP Process Integration Runtime".

Kind regards

Martin

entry.title: Re: APM In-house banking Interest calculation
entry.content:
Hi Paula,

the account settlement takes place on a monthly basis. Within the in-house bank accounts (app 'manage in-house bank accounts') you find in section 'account balancing' when the next account settlement process takes place. In the section 'condition' you can check the valid from date of the interests you entered.

The interest calculation takes place once the account balancing date is reached via scheduling the end-of-day process or you can run only the account balancing report separately.

Best regards,

Sigi

entry.title: Re: 401 Unauthorized error in HTTPS sender adapter in sap...
entry.content:
Hello,

Do you assign already the role MessagingSend to your user?

If you are using SAP Cloud Integration on Cloud Foundry then you probably have noticed you cannot add the default ESBMessage Send (MessagingSend) role to your user.

You will need to create a role collection to support this. 

Add this new role collection the role MessagingSend and assign this role collection to your user..

Also you need a instance to Service "SAP Process Integration Runtime".

Kind regards

Martin

entry.title: Re: How to create a Routing Rule in SAP GRC for different...
entry.content: Hi there Debashish first of all thank you very much for your time!

entry.title: Re: The NEW New Service Connection Process for Energy and Water
entry.content:
Hi, 
at the beginning of the process, a builder and functional location is created. The builder will not be the IS-U business parter in many cases but could be create like an IS-U BP with an FI-CA contract account. The functional location is an IS-U connection object. This is part of the reference process I describe.
At the end of the process you can add more steps, it varies how and where this process ends and IS-U metering starts. If the work crew that installs the new connection also installs a meter, not just a socket, the IS-U device location and technical device installation should be created at the same time. If it is known, what type of premise, installation and PoD will be present, it could be added. Otherwise the new connection process ends and the utility waits for a customer request to start a utilities contract.

entry.title: How to post GR for a consumable material without PO and directly consume it in MIGO?
entry.content:
I need to post a GR for a consumable material in MIGO without a PO. The material should not be stored in inventory but should be directly consumed (expensed to a cost center or another account assignment object).

I understand that movement type 201 is for issuing from stock, but in this case, the material is not in stock. 501 allows GR without PO, but it does not directly consume the material.

Is there a standard movement type for this process in SAP?

If not, can you guide me on how to create a custom movement type to handle this scenario?

Thanks in advance!

Thank you,

Best Regards,

Alexandro.

entry.title: Increase JMS queue size in Integration Suite
entry.content:
This blog describes how to increase JMS queue size in Integration suite. A JMS queue size refers to the capacity of the queue to hold messages. This size can determine how many messages can be stored in the queue before any additional incoming messages are either rejected or cause alternative handling strategies like scaling or persistence to come into play.

Increasing the size of a JMS queue may be necessary for several reasons, primarily related to performance, reliability, and scalability.

1. Access integration suite tenant, click on "Integration suite" tab displayed on the webui as shown in the below screenshot:

2. You will be navigated to capabilities page as shown below: 

3. Scroll up, click on manage capabilities as shown below:

4. In Manage capabilities, you will see "Message queue details", click on edit:

5. Increase queue size upto maximum 100 and save the changes:

6. To verify the same, navigate to Monitor > Manage message queues

entry.title: Problems with empty date fields in Smarttable
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am trying to display a table with start/enddate and start/endtime in an Smarttable. Unfortunately it is possible that the enddate and the endtime are not set yet. In this case the fields are empty.

The Smarttable is not showing any data whatsoever. Not even other rows with filled enddate/endtime. As soon as i dont display the fields it shows everything again.

The app is a Fiori Elements app with a custompage, where the smartable is implemented. It contains the ChildEntities of the Main Project.

<f:DynamicPage id="dynamicPage" fitContent="true">
                    <f:header>
                        <f:DynamicPageHeader>
                            <bar:SmartFilterBar
                                id="smartFilterBar"
                                entitySet="EntitySet">
                                <bar:controlConfiguration>
                                    <bar:ControlConfiguration
                                        key="StartDate"
                                        visibleInAdvancedArea="true"
                                        conditionType="{
                                            module: 'sap.ui.comp.config.condition.DateRangeType',
                                            defaultOperation: 'TODAY'
                                        }"
                                    />
                                </bar:controlConfiguration> 
                                <bar:layoutData>
                                    <FlexItemData shrinkFactor="0"/>
                                </bar:layoutData> 
                            </bar:SmartFilterBar>
                        </f:DynamicPageHeader>
                    </f:header>
                    <f:content > 
                        <smartTable:SmartTable
                            id ="SmartTable"
                            entitySet="EntitySet"
                            smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
                            tableType="ResponsiveTable"
                            enableExport="true"
                            beforeExport="onBeforeExport"
                            useVariantManagement="true"
                            useTablePersonalisation="true"
                            header="{i18n>JournalTable}"
                            showRowCount="true"
                            persistencyKey="SmartTableAnalytical_Explored"
                            enableAutoBinding="true"
                            demandPopin="true"
                            class="sapUiResponsiveContentPadding"
                            enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
                            tableBindingPath="to_Child"
                            beforeRebindTable="onBeforeRebindTable"
                            initiallyVisibleFields="WBSElement,CategoryName,Trade,Workpackage,Workstep,StatusName,Partner,StartDate,StartTime,EndDate,EndTime,PMUser,WorkType,CommentText,Finding,Organisation,WorkRessource,hasAttachments"
                            requestAtLeastFields="ProjectInternalID,HeadID,PositionID"
                        >
                        </smartTable:SmartTable>
                       
                    </f:content>
                </f:DynamicPage>

One solution is to cast the fields into character fields in the cds-View. Then the smarttable has no problems with initial fields. But i have to convert date and time according to the conventions of the User.

Does anyone has a similiar usecase? Do i overlook something simple?

Thank you for your help.

Best Regards Maximilian

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Vendor Condition Contract settlement error 0P2
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Re-valuation of order for FG & SFG with price control V?
entry.content: To enable CKMLCP (Actual Costing) to pick moving average price materials, you need to ensure that the Material Ledger is activated and the Valuation Area is correctly configured for the moving average price type. Additionally, check that the Price Control field is set to V for moving average price materials in the material master. If the client does not wish to use actual costing, you can settle orders using KKAO to update the WIP and variance without activating the material ledger.

entry.title: Re: TOP-DOWN
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike
